title: SPCA saves foal from open drain
bodyText:
Video

GEORGE NEWS & VIDEO - Inspectors from the Garden Route SPCA (GRSPCA) were called out to Delville Park yesterday, Monday 4 March, where a foal had fallen into an open drain.


> According to GRSPCA senior inspector Salomé Bruyns, the foal fell into the open drain as the drain cover had been stolen days prior to the incident.


"We are very thankful that it was a foal that fell in and not an adult horse. The foal we could still manage to pull out, but a grown horse would've been a completely different story. Luckily we managed to rescue the little fellow and return him to his mare," said Bruyns.

The foal sustained only minor injuries, cuts and bruises and was treated by the GRSPCA's veterinarian on the scene.

The foal was medically assessed by SPCA veterinarian Dr. Suzanne Muller.

Watch a video: Foal pulled out of open drain

Your browser does not support HTML5 video.

- Heartbreaking horse tale sparks international support
- Horse owners warned to be vigilant
- Horses targeted by cruel thieves

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
